task_completion.description: Import or read the NACA 2412 airfoil coordinates from the provided .dat file into FreeCAD.
task_completion.visual_evidence: FreeCAD interface showing the imported points in the 3D view, a macro/script being executed to read 'naca2412.dat', or an import dialog selecting the file.

task_completion.description: Connect the imported coordinates to form a closed 2D airfoil profile.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A continuous, closed curve in the shape of an airfoil (asymmetric, slightly cambered) visible in the FreeCAD 3D view. The Combo View (tree view) should show a Sketch, Wire, DWire, or Face object.

task_completion.description: Extrude the 2D airfoil profile to create a 3D solid wing segment with a 200mm length.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D solid object resembling a wing section in the FreeCAD 3D view. The tree view should show an 'Extrude' or 'Pad' operation. Ideally, the property panel shows a length or 'Along normal' value of 200.0 mm.

task_completion.description: Save the FreeCAD project as 'wing_section.FCStd' in the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FreeCAD window title bar or tree view root showing 'wing_section', or a save dialog confirming the file is being saved as 'wing_section.FCStd' in the '~/Documents/FreeCAD/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the FreeCAD GUI to perform the modeling tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the FreeCAD application open with active interaction (mouse clicks, menus, toolbars, or the built-in Python console) rather than just terminal commands executed headlessly.

integrity.description: The 3D model is genuinely constructed from the data, not an imported pre-made 3D file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The FreeCAD tree view (Combo View) shows the construction history (e.g., points, sketch/wire, extrude/pad) rather than a single imported solid, mesh, or STEP file.

integrity.description: The agent used the provided 'naca2412.dat' file rather than approximating the shape manually.
integrity.visual_evidence: Evidence of the file 'naca2412.dat' being selected in an import dialog, referenced in the FreeCAD Python console, or opened in a macro/script editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a 3D wing segment in FreeCAD using the NACA 2412 airfoil data. The expected chord length is 100mm, and the extrusion (span) must be exactly 200mm along the Z-axis. The final file must be saved as 'wing_section.FCStd'. The airfoil shape should visually reflect a NACA 2412 profile (asymmetric, 12% thickness ratio, slight camber). Look for a Pad or Extrude operation in the FreeCAD tree view with a length parameter of 200mm.
